# Hyperforce - Full Documentation

# Configuring Mule Properties to Access Control Planes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

By default, Mule runtime engine (Mule) is configured to connect to the US Cloud control plane. To
use Mule in other clouds, you must:

- Redefine the configuration properties that enable Mule to point to the correct control plane.
- Provide the
  [client ID and client secret](../access-management/managing-business-groups#client-id-and-secret)
  of the environment belonging to your organization.

If you do not override these default properties, the API gateway capability of Mule is disabled in
those cloud regions.

You can configure Mule properties using a `wrapper.conf file`, the command line, or CloudHub 2.0. To
configure Mule properties through Maven, refer to
[Publish assets using Maven](../exchange/to-publish-assets-maven#publish-an-asset-to-exchange-using-maven).

## Configure Mule Properties in the Wrapper.conf File

To configure Mule in the `wrapper.conf` file, modify these properties, replacing `<n>` with an
integer value that is unique within `wrapper.conf`. Ensure that you use the supported region
endpoint domains:

- `eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com`
- `ca1.platform.mulesoft.com`
- `jp1.platform.mulesoft.com`

The following example shows the `wrapper.conf` file configuration for Canada Cloud
(`ca1.platform.mulesoft.com`):

wrapper.java.additional.<n>=-Danypoint.platform.client_id=<environment client ID>
wrapper.java.additional.<n>=-Danypoint.platform.client_secret=<environment client secret>
wrapper.java.additional.<n>=-Danypoint.platform.base_uri=https://ca1.platform.mulesoft.com
wrapper.java.additional.<n>=-Danypoint.platform.analytics_base_uri=https://analytics-ingest.ca1.platform.mulesoft.com

## Configure Mule Properties via the Command Line

To configure Mule properties via the command line, use the following options when starting Mule.
Note that `./` applies only to Unix environments. Ensure that you use the supported region endpoint
domains:

- `eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com`
- `ca1.platform.mulesoft.com`
- `jp1.platform.mulesoft.com`

This example shows the properties configuration for EU Cloud `eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com` via the
command like:

./mule -M-Danypoint.platform.client_id=<environment client ID> -M-
Danypoint.platform.client_secret=<environment client secret>
-M-Danypoint.platform.base_uri=eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com
-M-Danypoint.platform.analytics_base_uri=https://analytics-ingest.eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com

## Configure Mule Properties for CloudHub 2.0

When deploying to CloudHub 2.0, in the **Properties** tab of Anypoint Runtime Manager, include these
properties. Ensure that you also use the supported region endpoint domains:

- `eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com`
- `ca1.platform.mulesoft.com`
- `jp1.platform.mulesoft.com`

The following example shows the properties configuration for Japan Cloud `jp1.platform.mulesoft.com`
:

anypoint.platform.client_id=<environment client ID>
anypoint.platform.client_secret=<environment client secret>
anypoint.platform.base_uri=https://jp1.platform.mulesoft.com
anypoint.platform.analytics_base_uri=https://analytics-ingest.jp1.platform.mulesoft.com

## See Also

- [Deploying Mule Apps](./)
---

# Hyperforce Release Notes

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

These are the release notes for Salesforce Hyperforce (Hyperforce).

## April 29, 2026

### Whatâs New

- Anypoint Partner Manager is now available for users in the US Cloud and EU Cloud.
- These Agent Fabric and Agent Visualizer features are now available in Canada Cloud and Japan
  Cloud.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Category</th><th>Feature</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td rowspan="4"><p>Discoverability</p></td><td><p>Agent Registry: Agent Assets</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Agent Registry: MCP Assets (Including Public MCP Assets)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Agent Registry: LLM Assets</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Agent Scanners</p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="6"><p>Governance</p></td><td><p>Agent, MCP, LLM instances in API Management</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>A2A, MCP, LLM Flex policies</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Managed Flex Gateway in CloudHub 2.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Managed Flex Gateway in Anypoint Runtime Fabric</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Trusted Agent Identity</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>MCP Bridge</p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><p>Orchestration</p></td><td><p>Agent Broker authoring with MuleSoft Vibes (Anypoint Code Builder)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Agent Broker authoring via YAML (Anypoint Code Builder)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Agent Broker deployment to CloudHub 2.0</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Agent Broker deployment to Anypoint Runtime Fabric</p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><p>Observability</p></td><td><p>Agent Visualizer nodes</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Agent Visualizer runtime edges</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Agent Visualizer: Agent Metrics</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

For details, see
[Agent Fabric Release Notes](../release-notes/agent-fabric/agent-fabric-release-notes) and
[Agent Visualizer Release Notes](../release-notes/agent-visualizer/agent-visualizer-release-notes).

## March 30, 2026

### Whatâs New

- Anypoint Insights dashboards introduce new charts and visualizations of metrics in EU, US, Japan
  (JP), and Canada (CA) cloud instances of Anypoint Platform on the Hyperforce infrastructure. These
  app and API metrics bring Anypoint Insights dashboards into near parity with metrics displayed
  through the built-in dashboards in Anypoint Monitoring.

  For details, see
  [Anypoint Monitoring Release Notes](../release-notes/monitoring/anypoint-monitoring-release-notes#march-30-2026).

## January 30, 2026

### Whatâs New

- In Canada Cloud and Japan Cloud instances of Anypoint Platform, Telemetry Exporter is now
  available to organizations with the Advanced subscription tier. Centralize observability with
  Telemetry Exporter by exporting audit log and trace data to your third-party monitoring ecosystem.

  Telemetry Exporter:
  [Exporting Telemetry Data to Third-Party Monitoring Systems](../monitoring/telemetry-exporter).

  For information about subscription tiers, see
  [Anypoint Platform Packages and Plans](../monitoring/#billing).

## December 18, 2025

### Whatâs New

- [MuleSoft Accelerator for Healthcare](../healthcare/latest/) and
  [MuleSoft Accelerator for Life Sciences](../life-sciences/latest/) are now available for users in
  the Canada Cloud.

## August 1, 2025

### Whatâs New

- Anypoint Partner Manager is now available for users in the Canada and Japan Cloud.

## December 9, 2024

### Whatâs New

- MuleSoft is now available on Hyperforce. You can deploy to Canada and Japan control planes.  
  For more information, refer to [Salesforce Hyperforce (Hyperforce)](./).

## See Also

- [Known Issues](https://help.salesforce.com/s/issues)
---

# Salesforce Hyperforce Overview

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Salesforce Hyperforce (Hyperforce) provides a modern infrastructure that lets you deploy MuleSoft
applications globally with control over data residency and compliance. Deploy your Mule apps rapidly
and reliably to locations worldwide while benefiting from enhanced security, scalability, and
privacy standards. Hyperforce offers a globally distributed platform that ensures optimal
performance for your integrations and APIs.

Some key benefits:

- Data Residency  
  Enables you to serve your users globally while maintaining control over data residency and
  compliance.
- Scalability  
  Enables you to grow and scale your business more flexibly and sustainably.
- Security  
  Follows least-privileged control, zero-trust principles, and encrypts data both at rest and in
  transit.
- Agility  
  Minimizes downtime for releases and maintenance. Additionally, it accelerates development and
  testing environments and offers extensive interoperability with AWS, streamlining your workflow.
- Privacy  
  Provides you privacy standards that give you control and transparency over your users' data.

## Features Supported on Hyperforce

This table indicates the support status of Anypoint Platform features on Hyperforce:

- Available: Feature is currently available on Hyperforce.
- Planned: Feature is scheduled for a future release on Hyperforce.
- Not planned: Feature is not currently planned for release or is not supported on Hyperforce.

Cloud</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td rowspan="4"><div><div><p><a href="../access-management/">Access Management</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>All other MQ Connector earlier than 4.x</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Planned</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Planned</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not planned</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not planned</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Anypoint MQ Connector versions 4.x and Apps</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Planned</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Planned</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not planned</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not planned</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>CloudHub 1.0 Deployments</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Planned</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Planned</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not planned</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not planned</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Connected ## Control Plane Regions

This table indicates the available and planned cloud regions for Hyperforce:

- Available: Cloud region is available on Hyperforce.
- Planned: Cloud region is scheduled for future support on Hyperforce.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Control Plane</th><th>Geographic Area</th><th>Platform URL</th><th>Status</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><a href="https://anypoint.mulesoft.com">US Cloud</a></p></td><td><p>North America</p></td><td><p><code>anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><p>Planned</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com">EU Cloud</a></p></td><td><p>Europe</p></td><td><p><code>eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><p>Planned</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://ca1.platform.mulesoft.com">Canada Cloud</a></p></td><td><p>North America</p></td><td><p><code>ca1.platform.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><p>Available</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://jp1.platform.mulesoft.com">Japan Cloud</a></p></td><td><p>Asia Pacific</p></td><td><p><code>jp1.platform.mulesoft.com</code></p></td><td><p>Available</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Mule Runtime Version Compatibility

- Hyperforce is compatible with Mule runtime engine versions under support, 4.4.0 and later.
- Hyperforce supports the Mule runtime engine LTS and Edge release channels. The recommended
  customer profile is LTS. For more information, visit
  [Edge and LTS Releases for Mule](../release-notes/mule-runtime/lts-edge-release-cadence).

## Deploy Mule Apps

When deploying Mule apps from any region control plane, you deploy on CloudHub 2.0 to a private or
shared space.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Region Control Plane</th><th>Deployment Configurations</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>US Cloud</p></div></div></td><td><p>Refer to <a href="../cloudhub-2/ch2-deploy">Deploying to CloudHub 2.0</a> and <a href="../cloudhub-2/ch2-architecture#shared-global-regions">Shared Global Regions</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Other Clouds</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>To deploy from other cloud regions, follow these tasks:<br></p></div><div><ol><li><p>Change the Anypoint Platform endpoints URLs to the region control plane endpoints:<br></p><div><ul><li><p><a href="configure-mule-hyperforce">Configure Mule to Access Control Planes</a></p></li><li><p><a href="../exchange/to-publish-assets-maven#publish-an-asset-to-exchange-using-maven">Publish an Asset to Exchange Using Maven</a></p></li><li><p><a href="../anypoint-code-builder/start-acb#change-clouds">Configure Anypoint Code Builder to a Different Control Plane</a></p></li><li><p><a href="../studio/hyperforce-configuration">Configure Anypoint Studio for Control Planes</a></p></li></ul></div></li><li><p><a href="../cloudhub-2/ch2-deploy">Deploy to CloudHub 2.0</a></p></li></ol></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Configuring Mule to Access Hyperforce](configure-mule-hyperforce)
- [Deploying Mule Apps to Hyperforce](./)
